title: Kaworu as John the Baptist
wikitext:
[]: Kaworu as John the Baptist?             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Thu Jul 19, 2007 7:08 pm After reading 'Shinji the Christ' by P J Knoetgen(^1), I thought maybe our favorite bishounen might be a little more bibilical than we think...Kaworu Nagisa, aka Tabris(Tabri a s), the Angel of Free Will. IIRC, Shin-Seiki( EDIT : My bad, it was Reichu) pointed out a 'Splitting of the Breast' reference in Episode 24, when the puppeted Nigoki stabs Sho in the breast with her prog knife. Just to reiterate- In the eponymous Episode 16, Shogoki enacts a nightmarish parody of birth out of the angel Leliel, blood spraying everywhere. While the bloodied Eva is being hosed down with high-pressure water, Ritsuko and Gendo are watching and talking. (In the previous episode, Rits gives a look that indicates obvious envy of Gendo's affection towards Rei). Episode 24, Sho's breast is literally split in the knife battle. After Kaworu's body is crushed and his head is popped off, the Eva is being hosed down, its hand bloodied. Rei takes Ritsuko's place at Gendo's side (And in Episode 23, Ritsuko is convinced she's just a cheap substitute for Rei, goes ballistic and destroys the dummy system) Aside from the way the Ritsuko/Rei messup is tied in, the bloody parody of birth and the subsequent hosing down in Episode 16 indicate baptism . Shogoki is baptised , and her sins (Leliel's blood) is washed away. And then Episode 24. Kaworu asks Shinji if he'll come along to the shower, and sure he does. Why is it that their first in-depth conversation, and Kaworu telling Shinji that he likes him unconditionally, take place in the shower? Baptism again...Kaworu 'The Baptist' has very subtly baptised Shinji 'The Christ'. John the Baptist was killed by decaptiation, Kaworu gets his head popped off (^2) (And presumably the severed head with Kaworu's soul in it got into GNR somehow). Once again, Shogoki is thoroughly hosed down in a second baptism. Rei takes Ritsuko's place as both part of the 'substitution' and to draw a visual parallel with the previous baptism. Last of all- Kaworu's identity. Tabris, or correctly, Tabri a s is Adam in human form, so he essentially is both the First and the Seventeenth(Last) Angel. From the Bible, the revelations of St. John the Divine, Chapter 1, Verse 17: "And when I saw him, I fell at his feet as dead. And he laid his right hand upon me, saying unto me, Fear not; I am the first and the last." Shinji falls at Kaworu's feet when both Evas come crashing down near Terminal Dogma, and Kaworu puts his right hand on Shinji's hand in the shower! And who is the first and the last? Yep. Verse 18 as well:"I am he that liveth, and was dead; and behold, I am alive for evermore, Amen; and have the keys of hell and of death. " Sure enough! Adam's old body was killed when it blew to bits, and only the embryo and Kaworu survived. Adam unleashed hell and death on Earth in the Second Impact. Kaworu himself says he could have lived forever with Nigoki. Need I draw any further parallels? And note the verse numbers. 1:17, 1:18. Mankind and Seventeen Angels(Counting Israfel, Ireul and Bardiel as one), a total of Eighteen Angels in all. Wonga! ^1: It's in the 'Written Statements' part of the Resources section on this site ^2: One more little detail: This is a lousy connection, but Shinji is adamant that Kaworu should have lived and that he was the better person. The Johannite cult believed that John the Baptist was superior to Jesus Christ (Leonardo da Vinci was a famous Johannite)

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Thu Jul 19, 2007 7:20 pm Well, the thought did pop into my head during those 62 seconds that Shoguki/Shinji held Tabris/Kaworu before smothering him "ZOMG, Kaworu is Jesus!" but I never thought of John the Baptist. Isn't there a thread on something like this already?

[]:             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Thu Jul 19, 2007 7:21 pm Oh wow, nice work.

[]: I might need Shin-seiki's help             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Thu Jul 19, 2007 7:57 pm Edit: Gotcha! One pillar image down. Eva commentary FTW! I guess I might need Shin-seiki's help for this one...how do you take screencaps in WMP? Edit 2: Thanks for the images, Shin-seiki. I just remembered about the 'twin pillars' symbolism that is seen both in Episodes 6 and 12. It's subtle, not repeated elsewhere as far as I know, but very telling if you take into account the account the twin pillars at the Temple of Israel. Episode 6 is the one with Operation Yashima, against Ramiel, 'Thunder of God', the Angel of divine visions and guide to the souls of the faithful. Ramiel was also called Uriel, 'City of God'- that might explain his/her high-tech appearance. As pointed out in the Da Vinci Code, Uriel is the guardian angel of John the Baptist, and I argue that Kaworu is our St. John figure. During the operation, Ramiel shoots at the same time Shinji does, and the positron beams from the Angel and the Rifle warp around each other in a double helix and the resultant explosion causes a two pillars of light- one green(Shinji's rifle) and one purple (Ramiel's weapon). At the start of Episode 12, the flashback to Misato at 14 and the hellish Second Impact, it ends with Misato opening the capsule and seeing two pillars of light rising- just like in the battle with Ramiel! Second Impact, of course, was also the event in which Kaworu was born. The twin pillars in the Great Temple of Israel at Jerusalem represented the two messiahs of Israel- the kingly messiah, and the priestly messiah. (More Da Vinci Code stuff- check out Rosslyn Cathedral, which replicates the pillars) One more addition- this one is eerie...John's father Zechariah(a priest) was struck mute because he refused to believe the Angel who told him he would have a son, John. Misato becomes mute(before the two pillars, nonetheless), although her case is not divine punishment but because of the horror of it all. The two pillars are therefore symbolic of Kaworu's birth as the Baptist (and the priestly messiah), and seeing the same with Ramiel is a deliberate visual link. Even if Ramiel didn't actually guard over Kaworu when he was floating in the Antarctic Dead Sea or elsewhere, there is a definite symbolic link between them, and Ramiel, with an immensely powerful AT field and a shoot-at-sight beam attack, would make for one hell of a guardian angel.

[]: Re: Kaworu as John the Baptist?             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jul 26, 2007 6:44 pm UrsusArctos wrote: IIRC, Shin-Seiki pointed out a 'Splitting of the Breast' reference in Episode 24, when the puppeted Nigoki stabs Sho in the breast with her prog knife.

[]: Re: I might need Shin-seiki's help             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Thu Jul 26, 2007 9:12 pm UrsusArctos wrote:

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Thu Jul 26, 2007 9:44 pm Actually, I'd been going through the Eva Commentary pages when I saw the image, and I immediately connected with the twin beams of light going up in Operation Yashima. There's one image of Adam's wings that looks suspiciously like beams of light, but from the looks of things, they're described in the script as that-'beams of light', and not Adam's wings, although a linked image in Renewal shows red dots (souls) flying around the beams and the wings. http://www.evacommentary.org/episode-12/episode-12A-scene1.html The wings spreading happens in cut 013A, while the pillars/beams of light happen in 017, which is much later. In any case, if those are the origins of the wings, either the description is a bit wierd or there is some sort of hidden significance to the wings beginning as beams of light. (Or I'm reading too deeply into this?)

[]: Re: I might need Shin-seiki's help             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Aug 31, 2007 9:54 pm CorporalChaos wrote: Wasn't this just Adam's 'wings' popping out of the ground and not a true "explosion" or am I just nitpicking?

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sat Sep 01, 2007 12:00 am This is indeed an interesting connection. But what would be the thematic point of making Kaworu into John the Baptist? How does it advance either the story or its overall meaning or symbolism?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Sep 01, 2007 12:47 am
